focus: security: renderer escaping, CSP preservation, hostile-content handling, validator allowlist boundaries, and unsafe file/state operations

findingDetails.title: Crafted snapshot manifest makes cleanup unlink arbitrary files outside the state store
findingDetails.scopeProof: The only destructive unlink at lines 1447-1448 consumes an unvalidated manifest blob value assembled at lines 1431-1444; no cleanup test or containment check was found, and a contained reproduction deleted a victim outside the state store.
affectedSurfaceHints: all user-writable files reachable by the process
findingDetails.claim: cleanup trusts manifest blob paths, allowing a crafted snapshot manifest to unlink a writable file outside the selected state store
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched for schema checks, basename restrictions, resolved-path containment, symlink/path-traversal defenses, mandatory dry-run, and an explicit trusted-store precondition; none was found. Dry-run is optional.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The store is intended to be local and disposable, and exploitation requires a crafted/corrupt manifest plus a real cleanup invocation; this lowers reachability but does not contain deletion to the store.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only after current code rejects non-basename blob values and proves the resolved target remains beneath the selected snapshot subdirectory, or authoritative threat-model evidence makes all accepted stores integrity-protected.
findingDetails.hunter: Traced all manifest-derived filesystem joins and isolated the destructive sink.
findingDetails.skeptic: Tested the local-only, disposable-store, and optional-dry-run explanations against source and docs.
findingDetails.referee: Executable reproduction proved deletion outside the store; destructive data loss sustains P0.

carriedAdjudications.claim: Unsupported cqi makes the variable-backed consuming declarations invalid instead of selecting the clamp bounds, violating the specified fixed-size fallback.
carriedAdjudications.counterevidenceSought: Checked for fixed fallback declarations, @supports guards, and containment by the fixed body font. The body rule prevents total loss of readable text but does not preserve prior per-surface sizes.
carriedAdjudications.alternativeExplanation: Modern Chromium IDE webviews support container query units, confining the defect to the promised unsupported-engine path.
carriedAdjudications.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if the fallback requirement is removed or engine-level evidence proves unsupported cqi in these var()-substituted clamp() values resolves to the bounds.

noveltyJustification: One new P0 contributes weight 10 against prior accumulated weight 6 plus current weight 10: 10/16=0.625. P1-001 is corrected carry-forward and contributes no new or refinement weight.
edgeCases: Exploit requires a crafted/corrupt manifest and non-dry-run cleanup.
Dry-run exposes but does not contain the escaped target.
Corrected P1-001 packet is administrative carry-forward, not a new finding.
